testcase.name: wifi_connect
testcase.description: Connect to WiFi and obtain DHCP address as prerequisite for throughput tests.
testcase.preconditions: WiFi interface is up; valid SSID/PSK configured; DHCP server available.

steps.step: Issue WiFi connect with configured credentials.
Verify interface is in COMPLETED state.
Wait for DHCP to assign IP address.

testcase.expected_result: WiFi connected; IP address obtained via DHCP.

testcase.name: tcp_upload
testcase.description: TCP upload throughput test using zperf API with default packet size.
testcase.preconditions: WiFi connected; iperf server running on SERVER_IP:SERVER_PORT.

steps.step: Configure upload params (server IP, port, duration, packet size 256).
Verify throughput meets minimum threshold (5 Mbps).

testcase.name: tcp_upload_large_packet
testcase.description: TCP upload with large packet size (1024 bytes) to evaluate throughput improvement.

steps.step: Configure upload params with packet size 1024.

testcase.name: udp_upload
testcase.description: UDP upload throughput test using zperf API with rate limiting.
testcase.preconditions: WiFi connected; iperf server running in UDP mode (-u).

steps.step: Configure upload params (packet size, target rate 10 Mbps).
Report throughput, packet loss, and jitter.
Verify throughput meets minimum threshold (3 Mbps).

testcase.expected_result: UDP upload completes; throughput >= MIN_UDP_THROUGHPUT_KBPS; loss reported.

testcase.name: udp_upload_large_packet
testcase.description: UDP upload with large packet size (1024 bytes).
testcase.preconditions: WiFi connected; iperf server running in UDP mode.

steps.step: Configure upload params with packet size 1024.

testcase.name: tcp_throughput_stability
testcase.description: Run multiple TCP uploads to verify throughput consistency.

steps.step: Verify min is at least half of minimum threshold.

testcase.expected_result: All iterations succeed; avg throughput >= threshold; variance is reasonable.

testcase.name: udp_packet_loss
testcase.description: Measure UDP packet loss and verify it remains below acceptable threshold.
testcase.preconditions: WiFi connected; iperf server running in UDP mode.

steps.step: Run UDP upload with configured rate.

testcase.expected_result: UDP packet loss is <= 10%; jitter reported.

testcase.name: tcp_upload_invalid_server
testcase.description: Attempt TCP upload to unreachable server; verify graceful failure.

steps.step: Verify either error return or zero bytes transferred.

testcase.expected_result: API returns error or zero throughput; no crash or hang.

testcase.name: udp_upload_zero_duration
testcase.description: Attempt UDP upload with zero duration; verify graceful handling.
testcase.expected_result: API handles zero duration gracefully; no crash.

testcase.name: wifi_disconnect
testcase.description: Disconnect from WiFi after all throughput tests complete.

environment.requirement: iperf server must be running on the configured SERVER_IP:SERVER_PORT. For TCP: iperf -s -p 5001 For UDP: iperf -s -u -p 5001
Device and iperf server must be on the same network subnet. DHCP server must be available.
